label: Six-CMA residential-property-price-to-income index
description: Archived broad new-and-resale property prices relative to national disposable income per person; the geography mismatch is explicit.
definition: Archived broad new-and-resale property prices relative to national disposable income per person; the geography mismatch is explicit.
comparisonLimitations: Country baskets, weights, index bases, seasonal treatment, and housing institutions differ. Compare growth rates and within-country percentiles before levels.
interpretation: A rising affordability ratio or growth gap means the documented cost is increasing relative to income or wages.
source: Derived from Statistics Canada; Derived from Statistics Canada; Statistics Canada
revisionStatus: Derived ratio. Normalized to 100 at 2017-01-01. Statistics Canada table 18-10-0169-01; inactive broad new-and-resale property index. The series is inactive and ends in 2021 Q4. It is not spliced to NHPI, resale, rent, or shelter CPI. Normalized to 100 at 2017-01-01. Income is a seasonally adjusted annual rate in millions of CAD and is not divided by four; population is the matching quarterly estimate. Statistics Canada table 36-10-0112-01 is latest-vintage and may revise recent or historical observations. Values are published at seasonally adjusted annual rates. Statistics Canada table 17-10-0009-01 is latest-vintage and may revise recent or historical observations.

calculation.formula: Rebased Six-CMA RPPI / rebased Canada disposable income per person × 100
calculation.explanation: Archived broad new-and-resale property prices relative to national disposable income per person; the geography mismatch is explicit.

limitations: Derived ratio. Normalized to 100 at 2017-01-01. Statistics Canada table 18-10-0169-01; inactive broad new-and-resale property index. The series is inactive and ends in 2021 Q4. candidateSymptoms: residential property prices diverging from income
